macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Dycom Industries (DY) Pre-Market Earnings Preview
items.summary: Dycom Industries is set to report Q1 2026 earnings with consensus EPS forecast of $1.66, a 49.55% YoY increase; the company has beaten expectations every quarter in the past year.

items.title: Bath & Body Works (BBWI) Earnings Expectations
items.summary: Bath & Body Works faces Q1 2026 consensus EPS of $1.77, down 15.31% YoY, after missing estimates by 12.5% in Q4 2025; 2026 P/E ratio at 7.45 vs industry 33.30.

items.title: Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) Strong Earnings Track Record
items.summary: ANF has beaten earnings expectations every quarter in the past year, with 2026 P/E ratio of 9.99 vs industry 8.90, signaling higher growth potential.

items.title: Daktronics (DAKT) Massive Earnings Growth Forecast
items.summary: Daktronics anticipates Q1 2026 EPS of $0.18, a 1700% YoY surge, ahead of its January 31, 2026 quarter-end report.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: White House Stablecoin Deadline Slips as Hoskinson Warns CLARITY Act Could Drive US Crypto Founders Offshore
items.summary: The White House has delayed its stablecoin deadline amid ongoing regulatory pushes. Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson warns that the CLARITY Act may force US crypto founders to relocate offshore.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 6.7% Amid Middle East Tensions, Crypto Platforms Used for Hedging Traditional Assets
items.summary: Bitcoin gained around 6.7% on March 2 following US strikes on Iran and escalating Middle East tensions, with traders using crypto exchanges for continuous commodity trading like oil and silver when traditional markets were closed.

items.title: Bitcoin Futures Markets Show Deep Deleveraging as Basis Compresses Despite 10% Price Rebound
items.summary: Bitcoin futures are experiencing significant deleveraging similar to late-cycle resets, even as the price rebounded 10% amid geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Brevis and Railgun to Discuss ZK Infrastructure and BNB Privacy in Live Talk Today
items.summary: Brevis (BREV) and Railgun (RAIL) will participate in a BNB Chain-hosted discussion on zero-knowledge infrastructure and native BNB privacy at 12:00 UTC on March 4, 2026.

items.title: March 2026 Events Poised to Shape Next Crypto Rally with Fed Decisions, CLARITY Act, and Bitcoin Nearing 20M Coins
items.summary: Key March events including Fed rate decisions on March 18, CLARITY Act progress, DC Blockchain Summit, Digital Asset Summit, and Bitcoin approaching its 20 millionth coin mined could influence markets, while altcoins remain under pressure.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Massey University Study Uses AI to Decode Brain Memory Recall Mechanisms
items.summary: Researchers at Massey University combined neuroscience and AI, using machine learning and deep-learning Transformer models to analyze brain activity, revealing how communication between brain regions changes as memories age from recent to remote. The findings highlight AI's role in neuroscience and potential applications for understanding Alzheimer's disease.

items.title: Sony AI Releases February 2026 Highlights Including Generative Audio Research and Responsible AI Keynotes
items.summary: Sony AI summarized February activities on March 2, featuring new generative audio research accepted to ICASSP 2026, responsible AI discussions in India, upcoming keynotes on reinforcement learning and responsible AI at UT Austin on March 3-4, and analysis of Sony's AI music detection methods.

items.title: Global AI Use Case Analysis Report 2026 Highlights Emerging Technologies Like GenAI, Edge AI, and QML
items.summary: A new research report released on March 3 analyzes AI use cases across industries, focusing on GenAI, edge AI, explainable AI (XAI), quantum machine learning (QML), LLMs, agentic AI, and reinforcement learning, with applications in robotics, cybersecurity, digital twins, and manufacturing.

items.title: 5 AI Automation Trends Predicted to Define Business Over Next 5 Years
items.summary: Key trends include autonomous decision-making systems for real-time operations, AI-augmented workforces enhancing productivity, and AI-driven quality/risk management with predictive analytics and automated compliance monitoring, as outlined in a March 3 analysis.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Highguard Live Service Shooter Permanently Shutting Down After One Month
items.summary: Wildlight Entertainment announced that multiplayer shooter Highguard will go offline after March 12, 2026, just a month after launch, citing failure to build a sustainable player base despite 2 million players. The studio laid off most staff post-launch and plans one final update.

items.title: Xbox Reveals March 2026 Game Pass Wave with Cyberpunk 2077 and Hollow Knight: Silksong
items.summary: Xbox Game Pass adds titles like Kingdom Come: Deliverance II (today), EA Sports F1 25 (tomorrow), Cyberpunk 2077 on March 10, and Hollow Knight: Silksong on March 12 for Premium subscribers. Final Fantasy III is also available today on select tiers.

items.title: Nintendo Indie World Showcase March 2026 Highlights Upcoming Switch and Switch 2 Titles
items.summary: Nintendo's 15-minute Indie World event revealed numerous indie games for Switch and Switch 2, including titles available today like Rotwood, with others like Denshattack! set for June 17, 2026. The showcase featured updates from independent developers across genres.

items.title: Xsolla Launches Unified SDK for Game Developers at GDC 2026
items.summary: Xsolla announced the global availability of its cross-platform SDK, consolidating PC, mobile, and web monetization tools including Payments, Login, Catalog, and Offerwall integrations to help developers launch and monetize games.

items.title: The Crew Motorfest Season 9 Launches NASCAR Collaboration and Trackforge Creator
items.summary: Ubisoft's The Crew Motorfest Season 9 begins March 4 with a NASCAR Motorfest Tour featuring 10 events, 16 vehicles, slipstream mechanics, and pit stops, plus the new Trackforge user-generated track creator and upcoming RC Frenzy playlist.

items.title: US Physical Video Game Sales Drop to $1.5 Billion in 2025
items.summary: New physical video game sales in the US fell to just $1.5 billion in 2025, continuing a nearly two-decade decline attributed to shifts toward digital distribution and publisher strategies.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Unveils New MacBook Air with M5 Chip at March 2026 Event
items.summary: Apple launched a new MacBook Air featuring the M5 chip, offering up to four times faster performance in certain tasks compared to the M4. Additional announcements include refreshed MacBook Pro models with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, new Apple Studio Displays, iPhone 17e, and M4 iPad Air with Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 6.

items.title: Arteris Network-on-Chip Technology Reaches 4 Billion Deployments Globally
items.summary: Arteris announced its network-on-chip technology has shipped in over 4 billion chips and chiplets worldwide, supporting high-end SoCs in compute, automotive, and data-intensive applications. The milestone highlights its role in addressing interconnect needs for complex multi-die architectures.

items.title: US Tech Giants to Sign Pact for Independent Power Grid for Data Centers
items.summary: Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Meta, Oracle, OpenAI, and xAI are set to formalize a compact on March 4, 2026, to fully fund new power generation and transmission for AI data centers under the Trump administration's Ratepayer Protection Pledge. Initiatives include Amazon's SMR funding, Microsoft's Three Mile Island restart, Google's geothermal deal, and Meta's hydrogen plants.

items.title: Broadcom Set to Report Q1 FY2026 Earnings with Focus on Semiconductor Growth
items.summary: Broadcom is expected to announce strong Q1 FY2026 results, driven by over 100% year-on-year growth in AI and ASIC revenue reaching about $8.2 billion, alongside growth in infrastructure software and networking products. Markets will assess its ability to sustain margins amid data center demand.
